title: Evidence-Guided: Creating High Impact Products in the Age of Uncertainty
excerpt: The book that made me rethink how I validate product decisions. Gilad's GIST framework replaced gut-feel roadmaps with a disciplined, evidence-driven system - and it's the closest thing I've found to a scientific method for product work.

title: Never Split the Difference: Negotiating as if Your Life Depended on It
excerpt: The negotiation book that actually works in product. Voss rewired how I handle stakeholder pushback, engineering constraints, and difficult customer conversations - by treating every negotiation as an exercise in tactical empathy rather than logical persuasion.

title: Understanding Michael Porter: The Essential Guide to Competition and Strategy
excerpt: The clearest explanation of Porter's strategy framework I've read. Magretta strips out the academic language and leaves the core: strategy is about being different, not better. That distinction reshaped how I think about AI product positioning.

title: Demand-Side Sales 101: Stop Selling and Help Your Customers Make Progress
excerpt: Moesta takes Jobs-to-be-Done from a product framework into a sales methodology. The core insight - that people don't buy products, they hire them to make progress in a specific situation - changed how I write product positioning and discovery interview guides.
